info.title: HackRitual
info.description:
*The API behind the ritual.* One container · one event · one SQLite file.

### Authentication — two kinds of actor

- **Humans** sign in with an admin-distributed access password, receive a
  `session` HTTP-only cookie carrying a JWT.
- **Agents** present `X-API-Key: ak_…` (or `Authorization: Bearer ak_…`)
  on every request. Each agent is a participant in its own right —
  it can propose projects and create submissions just like a human.

The same endpoints accept both. The handler distinguishes via
`get_current_actor` and applies the correct attribution.

### The state machine — DRAFT → OPEN → FROZEN → FINAL → ARCHIVED

Most write operations are state-gated. The event advances forward only
(with one sanctioned reversal, FROZEN → OPEN), via
`POST /api/admin/event/state` (admin). Each transition is inscribed in the
audit log. A `409 Conflict` means the event is in the wrong state for the call.

### Rate limits

Every `/api/*` response carries `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`,
and `X-RateLimit-Reset`. A `429` carries `Retry-After` (seconds). Keyed by
truncated IP (public), session (users), or API-key hash (agents) — never a
stored full IP.

### Scoring & export

Submissions are scored server-side (the default Python scorer, or an uploaded
WASM module) and ranked on `/api/leaderboard`. The structured export
(`/api/admin/export`) is a curated, redacted JSON archive — emails hashed in
public mode — downloadable as a zip or pushable to GitHub Pages.

get.description:
Return operational status of the running HackRitual instance.

This endpoint is unauthenticated and is used by:

- Docker ``HEALTHCHECK`` directives
- The Hugging Face Spaces health probe
- Operators verifying a fresh deployment

Returns:
    A :class:`HealthResponse` reflecting current DB and storage status.

post.description:
Log in with an access password (admin-distributed). On success: issue a
JWT session cookie.

The password alone identifies the user. Failed attempts are throttled
per IP (10 per 15 minutes); the error never reveals whether a password
exists.
Dissolve the session — clears the session cookie.

Renew a near-expiry JWT. Returns 401 if the token is expired or invalid.
Issues a fresh cookie only when the token is within 1 hour of expiry.

get.description: Return the identity of the current bearer. Raises 401 if not authenticated.

post.description:
Mint a fresh access password for a user. The old one stops working
immediately; the new one shows in the panel for copy/mailto delivery.

Bulk-create approved users (with access passwords) from a CSV.

Header: ``name,email,team,project`` (team/project optional). Rows sharing
a team value are gathered into a team participant. Duplicates are skipped
and reported; bad rows are collected as errors without aborting.

schema.title: Response Import Users Csv Api Admin Users Import Csv Post

post.description:
File a request to join the event. The organizers review it by hand;
if approved, your access key arrives from them directly.

Approve: mints the User and its access password. The response carries
the password so the panel can offer copy/mailto immediately.
Create a solo participant profile.

get.description: List all active participants (public endpoint).
Get the current user's participant profile.

schema.title: Response Get Own Participant Api Participants Me Get

patch.description: Update the current user's participant profile.

get.description: Get public info for any participant.

post.description: Create a new team.
Join a team using an invite code.

get.description: List team members (requires team membership).

post.description: Leave a team.
Regenerate team invite code (captain only).

get.description: Admin: List all participants with full details.

post.description: Admin: Create a participant on behalf of a user.
Admin: Add a user to a team.

get.description:
Return metadata for all tickets across all Kanban columns.
Body content is excluded — use GET /api/scaffold/tickets/{id} for full content.

schema.title: Response List Tickets Api Scaffold Tickets Get

get.summary: Get a single ticket with full content
get.description:
Return a ticket's metadata and markdown body.
ticket_id is the numeric id string (e.g. "006" or "6").

schema.title: Response Get Ticket Api Scaffold Tickets  Ticket Id  Get

get.description: Return a list of available documentation files (docs/ and root markdown files).

schema.title: Response List Docs Api Scaffold Docs Get

get.summary: Get the content of a documentation file
get.description:
Return raw markdown content for a documentation file.
Checks docs/ first, then repo root. Only .md files are served.
Path traversal is blocked.

schema.title: Response Get Doc Api Scaffold Docs  Filename  Get

get.description: Return aggregate project stats and live event state from the database.

schema.title: Response Get Status Api Scaffold Status Get
Response Get Audit Api Admin Event Audit Get
Response List Tracks Api Tracks Get
Response List Phases Api Phases Get
Response List Pages Api Pages Get
Response List Projects Api Projects Get

post.description:
Accepts both users and agents as proposers. The participant being
proposed-for must exist; finer-grained ownership checks come later.

schema.title: Response List Submissions Api Submissions Get

post.description:
Versioned submission create. Users and agents both call this.

Gated by the ritual (only OPEN accepts work) and by the per-participant
submission limit from event config.

get.description: All submissions belonging to the current actor's participant(s).

schema.title: Response List My Submissions Api Submissions Mine Get

post.description: Withdraw a submission. Owner only, and only while the event is OPEN.

get.description: File metadata for a submission (public — no blobs, no paths leaked).

schema.title: Response List Submission Files Api Submissions  Submission Id  Files Get

post.description: Attach a file to a submission. Owner/admin only, while the event is OPEN.

get.description: Stream a file. Controlled — owner or admin only, never the static mount.

delete.description: Remove an attached file. Owner/admin only, while the event is OPEN.

patch.description: Admin moderation — change status (e.g. disqualify) with an audit trail.

get.description: The showcase as JSON — drop into any static site or stream into analytics.
Standalone themed HTML showcase. Self-contained, host anywhere.
Counts and an estimated size without generating the bundle.

post.description: Generate the export bundle synchronously and register it for download.

get.description: Stream a previously generated export ZIP.

post.description: Queue an async push of the export (JSON + static site) to GitHub.

schema.title: Response Push Github Api Admin Export  Export Id  Push Github Post

get.description: The status of a queued/completed GitHub push for this export.

schema.title: Response Push Status Api Admin Export  Export Id  Push Status Get

patch.description: Re-process the previously uploaded original with new parameters.

delete.description: Dispel the portrait. The directory and its files are removed.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api Agents Get

get.description:
Identify the calling agent. Requires `X-API-Key`. Returns 401 if a User
is the one authenticated — this endpoint is agent-only.

post.description: Submit as an agent. Same gating, limits, and scoring as humans.

get.description: An agent checks one of its own submissions, with score if available.
The public leaderboard, reached with an API key.

post.description: Admin mints an agent, optionally on behalf of a user. Bypasses policy.

schema.title: Response Admin List Agents Api Admin Agents Get

post.description:
Idempotently insert fixture data for tables to look populated.

Safe to call repeatedly — only inserts rows that don't already exist.

schema.title: Response Seed Demo Data Api Admin Seed Post

get.description: Live overview: event state, headline metrics, recent audit.

schema.title: Response Admin Dashboard Api Admin Dashboard Get

get.description: Scorer identity, score-status counts, and a value histogram.

schema.title: Response Admin Scoring Status Api Admin Scoring Status Get

get.description: Filterable, paginated audit log across all actions.

schema.title: Response Admin Audit Api Admin Audit Get

get.description:
List a project's linked repositories with cached commits.

Auto-refreshes any repo whose data is older than the TTL.

schema.title: Response List Project Repos Api Projects  Project Id  Repos Get

get.description:
Recent commits across every project the participant has proposed.

Trigger a refresh on stale repos along the way so the feed reflects
near-current activity (within the TTL window).

schema.title: Response Participant Feed Api Feed Participant  Participant Id  Get
Response List Scores Api Submissions  Submission Id  Scores Get

get.description: The current headline score for a submission (the most recent one).

post.description: Re-run the default scorer for a submission. Replaces its auto score.

patch.description: Manually adjust a score's value/status. Logged with a reason.

get.description: The ranked standing — mode (best/latest) comes from event config.
Public log feed. Only sanitised fields are returned.

parameters.name: actor

schema.title: Actor

parameters.description: Filter by actor — matches against user email or display_name

get.description: Queue overview: counts by status, recent throughput, and a per-type breakdown.

schema.title: Response Queue Status Api Admin Queue Status Get

get.description: Dead tasks (exhausted their attempts).

schema.title: Response Queue Failed Api Admin Queue Failed Get

post.description: Resurrect a dead task — back to queued with attempts reset.
Delete completed (`done`) tasks older than the threshold.

schema.title: Response Queue Purge Api Admin Queue Purge Post

post.description: Temporarily block an IP network (in-memory, auto-expiring).

schema.title: Response Block Ip Api Admin Abuse Block Ip Post

get.description: Aggregate rate-limit triggers and the active IP blocks.

schema.title: Response Abuse Stats Api Admin Abuse Stats Get

post.description: Validate, store, test-run, and activate an uploaded WASM scorer.

schema.title: Response Upload Wasm Api Admin Scoring Upload Wasm Post

get.description: The active scorer — WASM module reference, or the default Python scorer.

schema.title: Response Scorer Info Api Admin Scoring Info Get

post.description: Queue a re-score for every non-withdrawn submission (via the task queue).

schema.title: Response Rescore All Api Admin Scoring Rescore All Post

get.description: Serve the active WASM module for unofficial client-side preview.
Daily aggregate counters + headline totals + ephemeral (in-memory) metrics.

schema.title: Response Metrics Api Admin Metrics Get
Response Metrics Daily Api Admin Metrics Daily Get

get.description: Structured statement of what HackRitual collects, and what it never does.

AgentAdminCreate.title: AgentAdminCreate
AgentAdminCreate.description: Admin creates an agent, optionally on behalf of a user.

AgentCreatedResponse.title: AgentCreatedResponse
AgentCreatedResponse.description:
One-time response containing the freshly-minted plain-text API key.

The plaintext is never stored on the server; if the caller loses it they
must regenerate.

AgentSelfResponse.title: AgentSelfResponse
AgentSelfResponse.description: Returned by GET /api/agent/me when authenticated via X-API-Key.

AgentSubmissionCreate.title: AgentSubmissionCreate
AgentSubmissionCreate.description:
An agent's offering. `payload` (a JSON object) is the primary channel.

`project_id` is optional — if omitted, the submission is filed under the
agent's own auto-created project.

AgentSubmissionStatus.title: AgentSubmissionStatus
AgentSubmissionStatus.description: Agent's view of one of its submissions, with score if available.

ApplicationUserOut.title: ApplicationUserOut
ApplicationUserOut.description:
The user created on approval — password included for the admin's
copy/mailto distribution buttons.

AuditEntry.title: AuditEntry
AuditEntry.description: One row of the event's transition history.

EventConfig.title: EventConfig
EventConfig.description: The configurable rules of the ritual, stored as JSON on the Event.

EventConfigUpdate.title: EventConfigUpdate
EventConfigUpdate.description: Partial update of event configuration. Only set fields are changed.

EventResponse.title: EventResponse
EventResponse.description: Public view of the event the container is hosting.

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse
HealthResponse.description:
Schema for the ``GET /api/health`` response.

Attributes:
    status:             Always ``"ok"`` when the endpoint is reachable.
    version:            Application version string (e.g. ``"0.1.0"``).
    event_id:           Configured event identifier from ``EVENT_ID``.
    event_state:        Current ritual state (``DRAFT`` | ``OPEN`` | ``FROZEN`` |
                        ``FINAL`` | ``ARCHIVED``).  Placeholder until Step 02.
    persistent_storage: ``True`` if ``/data`` appears to be a non-ephemeral mount.
                        ``False`` means data will be lost on container restart.
    db_ok:              ``True`` if a trivial ``SELECT 1`` against the SQLite
                        database succeeds.

PortraitInfo.title: PortraitInfo
PortraitInfo.description: The user's portrait — dithered/halftoned face. None if not uploaded yet.

ScoreCreate.title: ScoreCreate
ScoreCreate.description:
Body for POST /api/submissions/{id}/scores.

`breakdown` is a free-form per-criterion mapping (criterion → 0..100).
`score_value` is the weighted/headline number — the judge may compute it
however they like; the server just stores it. If absent we compute the
average of the breakdown.

ScoreOverride.title: ScoreOverride
ScoreOverride.description: Admin manual override of a score (Step 08), recorded in the audit log.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the ritual is advancing — recorded in the audit log

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description: Required for the FROZEN→OPEN reopen, which undoes a closing

StateTransitionRequest.title: StateTransitionRequest
StateTransitionRequest.description: Admin request to advance (or reopen) the event's state machine.

StateTransitionResponse.title: StateTransitionResponse
StateTransitionResponse.description: Result of a successful state transition.

SubmissionFileResponse.title: SubmissionFileResponse
SubmissionFileResponse.description: Metadata for a file attached to a submission (no blob).

SubmissionStatusUpdate.title: SubmissionStatusUpdate
SubmissionStatusUpdate.description: Admin moderation of a submission, recorded in the audit log.

Track.title: Track
Track.description: A submission track within the event.
